features.summary: Chevron reported strong Q3 2022 results with adjusted earnings up over $5 billion year-over-year, driven by higher upstream realizations and downstream refining margins. The company generated over $5 billion in free cash flow for the sixth consecutive quarter and returned more than $5 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. Permian production averaged 708,000 barrels per day, up 10% year-over-year, though expected to be at the lower end of 2022 guidance due to normalized drilling operations after depleting drilled-but-uncompleted well inventory.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q4 to see modest turnarounds and fewer LNG spot cargoes from Australia due to maintenance and seasonal factors. 2023 capital expenditure is planned at the top end of the $15-17 billion guidance range, representing approximately 20% increase from 2022 levels. The company maintains confidence in delivering 3% compound annual upstream production growth and expects to continue share repurchases at the top end of their guidance range while maintaining financial discipline across commodity cycles.
features.risks: Key risks include potential constraints at the CPC pipeline in Kazakhstan where two of three single-point moorings are offline for repairs, though full nominations have been secured for November. Global refining capacity remains tight with limited spare capacity, making the system vulnerable to unplanned outages or maintenance events. Inflation pressures are evident, particularly in the Permian Basin with low double-digit cost increases, and supply chain constraints may limit industry activity growth rates.
features.key_drivers: Primary earnings drivers include strong LNG performance with record cargo deliveries from Australia operations, robust refining margins in a tight global products market, and continued Permian Basin production growth. The renewable fuels business through the REG acquisition is performing above expectations with identified synergies and commercial opportunities. Affiliate dividends from TCO and Angola LNG are expected to reach the top end of guidance, supporting cash flow generation.
